area_anchor: Brikette — Email thread data can silently drift from expected format and admission records pile up
current_truth: Thread metadata_json has no schema validation on write — arbitrary data can be stored. Admission outcomes are recorded on every sync without dedup, accumulating duplicate rows.
next_scope_now: Add Zod schema validation for metadata_json writes and add upsert or unique constraint logic for admission outcomes.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: no metadata schema validation on write; duplicate admission outcomes accumulate
why: Without validation, the system can store unexpected data formats for email threads, which causes subtle bugs when the data is read later. Additionally, every time the sync runs it records a new admission decision even if one already exists for that thread, causing the database to accumulate unnecessary duplicate rows over time.

intended_outcome.statement: Metadata writes are validated against a Zod schema and admission outcomes use upsert logic to prevent duplicates.
